Lesser yin governs heaven, with great water as the central movement and yang brightness governing earth. The bingzi year is a year-conjunction year, and there is also the bingwu year. Its movement is cold; its transformation manifests as congealing, bleak, biting chill; its pathological excess manifests as ice, snow, frost, and hail; its disease manifests as cold below.
Great water, great wood, lesser fire, great earth, lesser metal.

In all years when lesser yin governs heaven in this manner, the transformation and movement of qi run ahead of the heavenly schedule. The qi of earth is stern and the qi of heaven is bright; cold intermingles with summer-heat, heat is added to dryness; clouds race toward the chambers of rain, the transformation of dampness circulates, and seasonal rain descends; metal and fire unite in virtue, corresponding above to the stars Yinghuo [Mars] and Taibai [Venus]. Its governance is bright and its ordinances sharp; its grains are the red and the white. Water and fire, cold and heat, contend within the qi-intersection, and this is where disease begins: heat disease arises above, cold disease arises below, and cold and heat encroach upon and contend within the middle. The people suffer cough and panting, overflow and discharge of blood, nasal obstruction, sneezing, red eyes with sores at the canthi, cold reversal entering the stomach, heart pain, lower back pain, abdominal distention, dry throat, and swelling above. In the first qi, as the qi of earth shifts, dryness is about to depart and cold begins; hibernating creatures return to their burrows, water freezes, frost again descends, and wind arrives; yang qi becomes stagnant, and the people, contrary [to expectation], guard themselves closely; the joints become rigid and restricted, and the lower back and buttocks ache; blazing summer-heat is about to arise, and sores erupt both within and without. In the second qi, yang qi spreads abroad, wind circulates, the qi of spring is rectified, and the myriad things flourish in response; cold qi arrives from time to time, and the people are at ease, though they suffer painful urinary dribbling, blurred vision, red eyes, and heat from qi stagnating above. In the third qi, the governance of heaven is displayed, the great fire [of summer] circulates, and the multitude of creatures flourish luxuriantly; cold qi arrives from time to time, and the people suffer qi reversal, heart pain, alternating cold and heat, cough, panting, and red eyes. In the fourth qi, sultry summer-heat arrives, great rains fall at their season, and cold and heat arrive by turns; the people suffer alternating cold and heat, dry throat, jaundice, nasal obstruction, nosebleed, and fluid-retention disorders. In the fifth qi, the dreaded fire [of the interim] presides and summer-heat unexpectedly returns; yang then transforms, and the myriad things grow, mature, and flourish; the people are healthy, though warm disease may occur. In the final qi, the ordinance of dryness circulates, and the remaining fire is blocked within, causing swelling above; coughing and panting, in severe cases, bring overflow of blood; cold qi repeatedly asserts itself, so that dense fog and mist obscure [the sky]; disease arises in the skin and interstices, lodges internally in the ribsides, and extends downward to the lesser abdomen, producing cold in the interior as the earth [qi] is about to change. One must restrain the excessive movement-qi and draw support from what the year's qi overcomes, breaking the outbreak of stagnant qi and first drawing upon the source of transformation, so as not to let sudden excess give rise to disease. Eat the grain proper to the year to preserve the true qi, and eat the supplementary grains to ward off vacuity evils. In such years the salty flavor should be used to soften, thereby regulating the upper [body]; in severe cases use the bitter flavor to release, and the sour flavor to astringe, thereby settling the lower [body]; in severe cases use the bitter flavor to discharge. Adjust according to whether the qi coincides or diverges, using more or less accordingly: where it coincides with the heavenly qi, use cold- and coolness-natured transformation; where it coincides with the earthly qi, use warmth- and heat-natured transformation. When employing hot remedies avoid hot seasons; when employing cool remedies avoid cool seasons; when employing warm remedies avoid warm seasons; when employing cold remedies avoid cold seasons — diet should follow the same rule. Where there is a false [semblance], act contrary to this — such is the Way. To act contrary to this principle brings on disease.
The Emperor said: Good. What is the governance of reverting yin [as controller of heaven]? Qibo replied: This is the chronicle of the si and hai years [the snake and pig earthly branches].

Reverting yin governs heaven, with lesser wood as the central movement and lesser yang governing earth; coolness and heat, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern, equivalent to an unmodified great-wood year. Both the dingsi and dinghai years are heaven-conjunction years; the movement progresses from wind to coolness to heat.
Lesser wood, great fire, lesser earth, great metal, lesser water.

Reverting yin governs heaven, with lesser fire as the central movement and lesser yang governing earth; cold and rain, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern. In the guisi and guihai years, the movement progresses from heat to cold to rain.
Lesser fire, great earth, lesser metal, great water, great wood.

Reverting yin governs heaven, with lesser earth as the central movement and lesser yang governing earth; wind and coolness, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern, equivalent to an unmodified great-wood year. In the jisi and jihai years, the movement progresses from rain to wind to coolness.
Lesser earth, great metal, lesser water, lesser wood, great fire.

Reverting yin governs heaven, with lesser metal as the central movement and lesser yang governing earth; heat and cold, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern, equivalent to an unmodified great-wood year. In the yisi and yihai years, the movement progresses from coolness to heat to cold.
Lesser metal, great water, great wood, lesser fire, great earth.

Reverting yin governs heaven, with lesser water as the central movement and lesser yang governing earth; rain and wind, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern. In the xinsi and xinhai years, the movement progresses from cold to rain to wind.
Lesser water, lesser wood, great fire, lesser earth, great metal.

In all years when reverting yin governs heaven in this manner, the transformation and movement of qi run behind the heavenly schedule; but in those years that are equivalent to an unmodified [central movement], the transformation and movement of qi run in step with heaven. The qi of heaven is turbulent while the qi of earth is upright; wind arises from the heights and distances, and blazing heat follows it; clouds hasten toward the chambers of rain, and the transformation of dampness then circulates; wind and fire unite in virtue, corresponding above to the stars Suixing [Jupiter] and Yinghuo [Mars]. Its governance is unsettled and its ordinances swift; its grains are the blue-green and the red; among the supplementary grains, that governed by the greater [movement dominates], and its depleted creatures are the striped-horned and feathered species. Wind, dryness, fire, and heat alternate in ascendancy and revenge; hibernating creatures emerge, and flowing water does not freeze; heat disease circulates below, wind disease circulates above, and the ascendancy and revenge of wind and dryness take shape within. In the first qi, cold begins to turn stern and killing qi is about to arrive; the people suffer cold below on the right side. In the second qi, cold does not depart; snow blossoms and ice forms on the water, killing qi exerts its transformation and frost descends, so that fragrant plants scorch at their tips; cold rain arrives repeatedly, yang returns to transform, and the people suffer heat within. In the third qi, the governance of heaven is displayed, wind arises from time to time, and the people suffer weeping, ringing in the ears, dizziness, and tremor. In the fourth qi, sultry heat and damp-heat contend with one another above on the left side; the people suffer jaundice and puffy swelling. In the fifth qi, dryness and dampness alternate in dominance, deep overcast spreads, cold qi reaches the body, and wind and rain circulate. In the final qi, the dreaded fire [of the interim] takes command, yang undergoes great transformation, hibernating creatures emerge, flowing water does not freeze, the qi of earth bursts forth abundantly, plants grow, and people are at ease, though pestilential warm disease may occur. One must break the stagnant qi, draw upon the source of transformation, and assist the movement-qi, so as not to let evil qi prevail. In such years the acrid flavor should be used to regulate the upper [body] and the salty flavor to regulate the lower; one must not rashly violate the dreaded fire qi. When employing warm remedies avoid warm seasons; when employing hot remedies avoid hot seasons; when employing cool remedies avoid cool seasons; when employing cold remedies avoid cold seasons — diet should follow the same rule. Where there is a false [semblance] contrary to the norm, such is the Way; to act contrary to this brings disease.

The Emperor said: Good. Master's words may be called complete indeed. Yet how are their manifestations to be discerned? Qibo said: How illuminating is this question! The six qi proceed in sequence and rest in [fixed] positions; therefore one customarily observes them at dawn on the first day of the first month, and by observing their position one may know where they are. When a movement is in excess, its arrival comes early; when a movement is insufficient, its arrival comes late — this is the Way of heaven, the constancy of qi. When a movement is neither excessive nor insufficient, this is called a proper year, and its arrival coincides exactly with its due time. The Emperor asked: The qi of ascendancy and revenge is ever-present, yet calamities and portents arrive only at certain times — how is this? Qibo said: What does not accord with the [expected] transformation of qi is called a calamity.

The Emperor asked: How do the numbers of heaven and earth begin and end? Qibo said: How thorough is this question! This is to clarify the Way indeed. The numbering begins above and ends below: before the midpoint of the year, the qi of heaven governs; after the midpoint of the year, the qi of earth governs; where upper and lower intersect, the qi-intersection governs — and thus the chronicle of the year is complete. Hence it is said: once the position is clear, may the month of the qi be known? This is what is meant by 'qi.'

The Emperor asked: I have taken charge of this matter and applied it accordingly, yet it does not always accord with these numbers — why is this? Qibo said: The application of qi has its greater or lesser degree, and the harmony of transformation has its flourishing or decline; whether declining or flourishing, greater or lesser, [these years] nonetheless share the same transformation. The Emperor said: I wish to hear of what is meant by 'sharing the same transformation.' Qibo said: Wind and warmth share the transformation of spring; heat, sultriness, and gloom share the transformation of summer fire, whether as ascendancy or as revenge; dryness, coolness, mist, and dew share the transformation of autumn; clouds, rain, dimness, and dust share the transformation of late summer; cold qi, frost, snow, and ice share the transformation of winter — these are the constant transformations of the five movements and six qi of heaven and earth, alternating in application according to flourishing and decline. The Emperor said: When the five movements coincide with heaven's transformation, this is called a heaven-conjunction [tianfu] year — this I already know. I wish to hear of what is meant by coinciding with earth's transformation. Qibo said: There are three years in which excess coincides with heaven's transformation, and likewise three in which insufficiency coincides with heaven's transformation; there are three years in which excess coincides with earth's transformation, and likewise three in which insufficiency coincides with earth's transformation — twenty-four years in all. The Emperor said: I wish to hear what these are. Qibo said: In the jiachen and jiaxu years, great earth [as central movement] has greater yin added below; in the renyin and renshen years, great wood has reverting yin added below; in the gengzi and gengwu years, great metal has yang brightness added below — these are three. In the guisi and guihai years, lesser fire has lesser yang added below; in the xinchou and xinwei years, lesser water has greater yang added below; in the guimao and guiyou years, lesser fire has lesser yin added below — these are three [more]. In the wuzi and wuwu years, great fire has lesser yin presiding above; in the wuyin and wushen years, great fire has lesser yang presiding above; in the bingchen and bingxu years, great water has greater yang presiding above — these are three. In the dingsi and dinghai years, lesser wood has reverting yin presiding above; in the yimao and yiyou years, lesser metal has yang brightness presiding above; in the jichou and jiwei years, lesser earth has greater yin presiding above — these are three [more]. Apart from these twenty-four years, there is neither 'adding' nor 'presiding.' The Emperor asked: What is meant by 'adding'? Qibo said: When excess is 'added,' this is the same as a heaven-conjunction year; when insufficiency is 'added,' this is the same as a year-conjunction year. The Emperor asked: What is meant by 'presiding'? Qibo said: Whether excess or insufficiency, [when the central movement coincides with the qi governing heaven] both are called heaven-conjunction years, though the degree of change and movement differs, the manifestations of disease vary in severity, and the timing of life and death comes early or late accordingly.

The Emperor said: Master has spoken of employing hot remedies while avoiding heat, and employing cold remedies while avoiding cold — I do not yet understand this; I wish to hear what is meant by 'avoiding.' Qibo said: [In seasons of] heat, do not violate [the principle of avoiding] heat; [in seasons of] cold, do not violate [the principle of avoiding] cold. To follow this brings harmony; to go against it brings disease. One must respectfully hold this in awe and avoid transgressing it — this is what is meant by the seasonal ascendancy of the six positions. The Emperor asked: What of warmth and coolness? Qibo said: When the governing qi is hot, do not violate it by using heat; when the governing qi is cold, do not violate it by using cold; when the governing qi is cool, do not violate it by using coolness; when the governing qi is warm, do not violate it by using warmth. When the interim qi accords with its ruling [seasonal] qi, do not violate it either; when it diverges from its ruling qi, one may violate it slightly — these are called the four things to be held in awe, and one must examine them carefully. The Emperor said: Good. What of when violation is permissible? Qibo said: When the qi of heaven runs contrary to the season, one may rely on the [ordinary] rule; when [the pathogenic qi] overcomes its ruling [seasonal] qi, one may violate [the principle of avoidance], taking balance as the goal, without going to excess — this applies to cases where evil qi has reversed and become dominant. Hence it is said: do not forsake the good faith of heaven, do not go against the fitness of qi, do not assist what is already ascendant, and do not aid what is already in revenge — this is called the perfection of governance.
The Emperor said: Good. Do the movements of the five phases governing the years have a constant numerical order? Qibo said: Allow your servant to set them forth in sequence.
The jiazi and jiawu years.

Above is lesser yin fire; the central movement is great earth; below is yang brightness metal. The transformation-number of heat is two; of rain, five; of dryness, four — these are called the days of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, salty and cold; in the middle, bitter and hot; below, sour and hot — these are called the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The yichou and yiwei years.

Above is greater yin earth; the central movement is lesser metal; below is greater yang water. Heat-transformation and cold-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — this is called a day of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the seventh palace. The transformation-number of dampness is five; of coolness, four; of cold, six — these are called the days of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and hot; in the middle, sour and mild; below, sweet and hot — these are called the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The bingyin and bingshen years.

Above is lesser yang ministerial fire; the central movement is great water; below is reverting yin wood. The transformation-number of fire is two; of cold, six; of wind, three — these are called the days of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, salty and cold; in the middle, salty and warm; below, acrid and warm — these are called the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The dingmao and dingyou years.

Above is yang brightness metal; the central movement is lesser wood; below is lesser yin fire. Coolness-transformation and heat-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — this is called a day of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the third palace. The transformation-number of dryness is nine; of wind, three; of heat, seven — these are called the days of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and slightly warm; in the middle, acrid and mild; below, salty and cold — these are called the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The wuchen and wuxu years.

Above is greater yang water; the central movement is great fire; below is greater yin earth. The transformation-number of cold is six; of heat, seven; of dampness, five — these are called the days of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and warm; in the middle, sweet and mild; below, sweet and warm — these are called the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The jisi and jihai years.

Above is reverting yin wood; the central movement is lesser earth; below is lesser yang ministerial fire. Wind-transformation and coolness-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — this is called a day of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the fifth palace. The transformation-number of wind is three; of dampness, five; of fire, seven — these are called the days of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, acrid and cool; in the middle, sweet and mild; below, salty and cold — these are called the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The gengwu and gengzi years.

Above is lesser yin fire; the central movement is great metal; below is yang brightness metal. The transformation-number of heat is seven; of coolness, nine; of dryness, nine — these are called the days of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, salty and cold; in the middle, acrid and warm; below, sour and warm — these are called the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The xinwei and xinchou years.

Above is greater yin earth; the central movement is lesser water; below is greater yang water. Rain-transformation and wind-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — this is called a day of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the first palace. The transformation-number of rain is five; of cold, one — these are called the days of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and hot; in the middle, bitter and mild; below, bitter and hot — these are called the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The renshen and renyin years.

Above is lesser yang ministerial fire; the central movement is great wood; below is reverting yin wood. The transformation-number of fire is two; of wind, eight — these are called the days of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, salty and cold; in the middle, sour and mild; below, acrid and cool — these are called the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The guiyou and guimao years.

Above is yang brightness metal; the central movement is lesser fire; below is lesser yin fire. Cold-transformation and rain-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — this is called a day of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the ninth palace. The transformation-number of dryness is nine; of heat, two — these are called the days of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and slightly warm; in the middle, salty and warm; below, salty and cold — these are called the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The jiaxu and jiachen years.

Above is greater yang water; the central movement is great earth; below is greater yin earth. The transformation-number of cold is six; of dampness, five — these are the days of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and hot; in the middle, bitter and warm; below, bitter and warm — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The yihai and yisi years.

Above is reverting yin wood; the central movement is lesser metal; below is lesser yang ministerial fire. Heat-transformation and cold-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — a day of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the seventh palace. The transformation-number of wind is eight; of coolness, four; of fire, two — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, acrid and cool; in the middle, sour and mild; below, salty and cold — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The bingzi and bingwu years.

Above is lesser yin fire; the central movement is great water; below is yang brightness metal. The transformation-number of heat is two; of cold, six; of coolness, four — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, salty and cold; in the middle, salty and hot; below, sour and warm — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The dingchou and dingwei years.

Above is greater yin earth; the central movement is lesser wood; below is greater yang water. Coolness-transformation and heat-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — degrees of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the third palace. The transformation-number of rain is five; of wind, three; of cold, one — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and warm; in the middle, acrid and warm; below, sweet and hot — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The wuyin and wushen years.

Above is lesser yang ministerial fire; the central movement is great fire; below is reverting yin wood. The transformation-number of fire is seven; of wind, three — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, salty and cold; in the middle, sweet and mild; below, acrid and cool — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The jimao and jiyou years.

Above is yang brightness metal; the central movement is lesser earth; below is lesser yin fire. Wind-transformation and coolness-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — degrees of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the fifth palace. The transformation-number of coolness is nine; of rain, five; of heat, seven — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and slightly warm; in the middle, sweet and mild; below, salty and cold — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The gengchen and gengxu years.

Above is greater yang water; the central movement is great metal; below is greater yin earth. The transformation-number of cold is one; of coolness, nine; of rain, five — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and hot; in the middle, acrid and warm; below, sweet and hot — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The xinsi and xinhai years.

Above is reverting yin wood; the central movement is lesser water; below is lesser yang ministerial fire. Rain-transformation and wind-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — degrees of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the first palace. The transformation-number of wind is three; of cold, one; of fire, seven — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, acrid and cool; in the middle, bitter and mild; below, salty and cold — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The renwu and renzi years.

Above is lesser yin fire; the central movement is great wood; below is yang brightness metal. The transformation-number of heat is two; of wind, eight; of coolness, four — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, salty and cold; in the middle, sour and cool; below, sour and warm — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The guiwei and guichou years.

Above is greater yin earth; the central movement is lesser fire; below is greater yang water. Cold-transformation and rain-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — degrees of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the ninth palace. The transformation-number of rain is five; of fire, two; of cold, one — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and warm; in the middle, salty and warm; below, sweet and hot — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The jiashen and jiayin years.

Above is lesser yang ministerial fire; the central movement is great earth; below is reverting yin wood. The transformation-number of fire is two; of rain, five; of wind, eight — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, salty and cold; in the middle, salty and mild; below, acrid and cool — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The yiyou and yimao years.

Above is yang brightness metal; the central movement is lesser metal; below is lesser yin fire. Heat-transformation and cold-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — degrees of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the seventh palace. The transformation-number of dryness is four; of coolness, four; of heat, two — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and slightly warm; in the middle, bitter and mild; below, salty and cold — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The bingxu and bingchen years.

Above is greater yang water; the central movement is great water; below is greater yin earth. The transformation-number of cold is six; of rain, five — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and hot; in the middle, salty and warm; below, sweet and hot — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The dinghai and dingsi years.

Above is reverting yin wood; the central movement is lesser wood; below is lesser yang ministerial fire. Coolness-transformation and heat-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — degrees of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the third palace. The transformation-number of wind is three; of fire, seven — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, acrid and cool; in the middle, acrid and mild; below, salty and cold — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The wuzi and wuwu years.

Above is lesser yin fire; the central movement is great fire; below is yang brightness metal. The transformation-number of heat is seven; of coolness, nine — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, salty and cold; in the middle, sweet and cold; below, sour and warm — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The jichou and jiwei years.

Above is greater yin earth; the central movement is lesser earth; below is greater yang water. Wind-transformation and coolness-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — degrees of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the fifth palace. The transformation-number of rain is five; of cold, one — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and hot; in the middle, sweet and mild; below, sweet and hot — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The gengyin and gengshen years.

Above is lesser yang ministerial fire; the central movement is great metal; below is reverting yin wood. The transformation-number of fire is seven; of coolness, nine; of wind, three — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, salty and cold; in the middle, acrid and warm; below, acrid and cool — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The xinmao and xinyou years.

Above is yang brightness metal; the central movement is lesser water; below is lesser yin fire. Rain-transformation and wind-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — degrees of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the first palace. The transformation-number of coolness is nine; of cold, one; of heat, seven — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and slightly warm; in the middle, bitter and mild; below, salty and cold — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The renchen and renxu years.

Above is greater yang water; the central movement is great wood; below is greater yin earth. The transformation-number of cold is six; of wind, eight; of rain, five — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, bitter and warm; in the middle, sour and mild; below, sweet and warm — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.
The guisi and guihai years.

Above is reverting yin wood; the central movement is lesser fire; below is lesser yang ministerial fire. Cold-transformation and rain-transformation, ascendancy and revenge, follow the same pattern — degrees of transformation under evil qi; calamity falls in the ninth palace. The transformation-number of wind is eight; of fire, two — the degrees of proper transformation. Its suitable flavors: above, acrid and cool; in the middle, salty and mild; below, salty and cold — the appropriate substances for medicine and diet.

In all these fixed-period chronicles, the ascendancy-and-revenge and the proper transformations each have their constant numbers, which must not go unexamined. Thus one who knows the essential can sum it up in a single phrase; one who does not know the essential wanders in endless dispersion — this is what is meant.

The Emperor said: Good. Do the qi of the five movements also have their revenge upon the year? Qibo said: When stagnation reaches its extreme, it then breaks forth, awaiting its proper time to act. The Emperor said: May I ask what this means? Qibo said: Among the qi of the five constants, excess and insufficiency each break forth differently. The Emperor said: I wish to hear this in full. Qibo said: What is excessive breaks forth violently, what is insufficient breaks forth slowly; the violent produces severe disease, the slow produces lingering disease. The Emperor asked: What of the numbers of excess and insufficiency? Qibo said: The excessive follow the 'completion' numbers, the insufficient follow the 'generation' numbers — earth constantly follows the generation number.

The Emperor asked: What are these outbursts like? Qibo said: When earth's stagnation breaks forth, cliffs and valleys are shaken with fright, thunder rumbles through the qi-intersection, dust darkens [the sky] with yellow and black, transforming into white vapor; sudden squalls sweep the heights and depths, striking rocks and hurling them through the air; floodwaters then rush forth, streams overflow and spread widely, and the fields and pastures become mired in earth. The transforming qi then spreads abroad, becoming favorable seasonal rain, so that growth begins, maturation begins, transformation begins, and completion begins. Thus the people suffer distention of the heart and abdomen, intestinal rumbling with frequent loose stools, and in severe cases heart pain, distention of the ribsides, vomiting, sudden turmoil disorder, fluid effusion with pouring discharge, puffy swelling, and heaviness of the body. Clouds race toward the chambers of rain, rosy clouds encircle the rising sun, and mountains and marshes darken with dust. This outburst occurs during the fourth [seasonal] qi [of the year]. When clouds stretch across the mountains of heaven, drifting and forming and dissolving, this is the first sign of impending stagnation-outburst. When metal's stagnation breaks forth, the heavens are clear and the earth is bright; wind is crisp and the qi is cutting; great coolness then arises; grasses and trees seem wrapped in floating mist, and dry qi circulates; dense fog rises repeatedly, killing qi arrives, plants and trees turn ashen and wither, and metal produces its [characteristic] sound. Thus the people suffer coughing counterflow, fullness of the heart and ribsides drawing into sudden pain in the lesser abdomen so severe that they cannot turn from side to side, dry throat, and a dusty, sickly complexion. Marshes emerge scorched and withered, and the earth congeals with frost and salt-crust. This stagnation-outburst occurs during the fifth [seasonal] qi. When night dew falls white and the forests and thickets sound mournful, this is the sign of impending stagnation-outburst. When water's stagnation breaks forth, yang qi is driven back and yin qi suddenly rises; great cold then arrives, streams and marshes freeze solid, and cold vapor congeals into frost and snow; in severe cases yellow-black haze obscures [the sky], circulating through the qi-intersection, and frost then kills [vegetation] — water thus manifests its portent. Thus the people suffer heart pain from invading cold, pain of the lower back and buttocks, dysfunction of the major joints, difficulty in bending and stretching, tendency to reversal [cold limbs], and hard focal distention with abdominal fullness. The light of yang fails to govern, and an empty accumulation of deep overcast, white dust, and darkness ensues before the outburst occurs — this happens around the second [seasonal] qi, before or after the [interposed] fire [qi]. When the great void grows deeply somber, the qi seeming to disperse like scattered hemp, faintly visible yet hidden, its color black tinged with faint yellow, this is the first sign of impending stagnation-outburst. When wood's stagnation breaks forth, the great void darkens with dust, clouds and vapors are thrown into turmoil, and great winds arrive, tearing roofs and snapping trees — wood undergoes its transformation. Thus the people suffer pain in the stomach cavity felt at the region of the heart, extending upward into both ribsides, obstruction of the diaphragm and throat so that food and drink cannot descend, and in severe cases ringing in the ears, dizziness and spinning vision, failure to recognize people, and sudden rigid collapse. When the great void turns blue-gray with dust, sky and mountains merging into one color, or when the qi turns turbid with a yellow-black hue, stagnating like a spread-out cloud that does not yet release rain, the outburst then occurs — its timing is irregular. When long rivers bend the grasses along their banks, tender leaves show their shaded undersides, pines moan on the high mountains, and tigers roar among the cliffs and caves, these are the first signs of impending stagnation-outburst. When fire's stagnation breaks forth, the great void swells with haze and the sun's brightness is obscured; blazing fire circulates and great summer-heat arrives; mountains and marshes seem to burn and scorch, timber oozes resin, great halls seem wreathed in smoke, the earth's surface floats with frost-salt, standing water diminishes, trailing vines and grasses turn scorched yellow, wind spreads confused rumor, and the transformation of dampness is thereby delayed. Thus the people suffer shortness of qi, sores and welling-abscess swelling in the ribsides, abdomen, chest, back, face, head, and four limbs, distention and fullness, sores and rashes, vomiting counterflow, tugging and slackening convulsions, bone pain, movement within the joints, diarrhea with warm malarial disorder, sudden abdominal pain, overflow and spreading of blood, diminished essence-fluids, red eyes, heat in the heart, and in severe cases clouded oppression and vexation, with a tendency to sudden death. When the marked hours draw to their end amid great warmth, sweat moistens the pores; the outburst then occurs during the fourth [seasonal] qi. Movement then returns to stillness, and yang at its extreme reverts to yin, so that the ordinance of dampness then transforms and completes. When blossoms open while water still lies congealed, when mountains and rivers show ice and snow even as the blazing sun scorches the noon marshes, these are the first signs of impending stagnation-outburst. There is always a manifest response to stagnation before its outburst comes as recompense; in every case one must observe its extreme point, whereupon the outburst occurs. Wood's outburst has no fixed time, for water follows in the wake of fire. If one carefully awaits its proper time, the [course of] disease can be anticipated; but if the timing is missed and the year runs contrary, the five qi fail to proceed in order, and generation, transformation, gathering, and storage lose their constancy in governance.

The Emperor asked: When water breaks forth there is hail and snow; when earth breaks forth there are sudden squalls; when wood breaks forth there is destruction and breakage; when metal breaks forth there is clear brightness; when fire breaks forth there is sultry gloom — what qi causes these? Qibo said: Qi has its greater and lesser degree, and outbursts have their mild and severe forms; the mild correspond to their own [governing] qi alone, while the severe combine with the qi that they overcome below — by examining that subordinate qi, [the pattern] can be discerned.

The Emperor said: Good. Why do the outbursts of the five qi sometimes fail to occur in their proper position? Qibo said: This is termed a 'deviation.' The Emperor asked: Does this deviation have a fixed number? Qibo said: It is always delayed by thirty-some degrees. The Emperor asked: What of when the qi's arrival comes early or late? Qibo said: When a movement is excessive its arrival comes early; when a movement is insufficient its arrival comes late — this is the constant pattern of observation. The Emperor asked: What of when it arrives exactly on time? Qibo said: When it is neither excessive nor insufficient, its arrival falls exactly on time; anything other than this is an aberration. The Emperor said: Good. What of when qi transforms out of season? Qibo said: The excessive transforms within its own proper time, while the insufficient reverts to [the time of] what overcomes it. The Emperor asked: The qi of the four seasons arrives early or late, high or low, left or right — what is the pattern of this? Qibo said: Its movement may be contrary or compliant, and its arrival may be slow or swift; thus the excessive transforms ahead of the heavenly schedule, while the insufficient transforms behind the heavenly schedule. The Emperor said: I wish to hear of this movement — what does it mean? Qibo said: The qi of spring moves westward, the qi of summer moves northward, the qi of autumn moves eastward, and the qi of winter moves southward. Thus the qi of spring begins below, the qi of autumn begins above, the qi of summer begins in the middle, and the qi of winter begins at the tip [periphery]; the qi of spring begins on the left, the qi of autumn begins on the right, the qi of winter begins behind, and the qi of summer begins in front — this is the constant pattern of the four seasons' proper transformation. Thus in high places the qi of winter constantly prevails, and in low places the qi of spring constantly prevails; one must examine this carefully. The Emperor said: Good.

The Yellow Emperor asked: What are the manifest correspondences of the five movements and six qi, the standard order of the six transformations, and the chronicle of the six changes? Qibo replied: The proper chronicle of the six qi encompasses transformation and change, ascendancy and revenge, application and disease — their manifestations are not uniform; which does the Emperor wish [to hear of]? The Emperor said: I wish to hear all of it. Qibo said: Allow me to set it forth in full. As for where the qi arrives: where reverting yin arrives, there is harmony and calm; where lesser yin arrives, there is warmth; where greater yin arrives, there is dust and sultry damp; where lesser yang arrives, there is blazing summer-heat; where yang brightness arrives, there is clear severity; where greater yang arrives, there is a chill vapor of cold — this is the constant pattern of seasonal transformation.

Where reverting yin arrives, it is the chamber of wind, causing cracks to open. Where lesser yin arrives, it is the chamber of fire, causing unfolding and flourishing. Where greater yin arrives, it is the chamber of rain, causing roundness and fullness. Where lesser yang arrives, it is the chamber of heat, causing outward movement. Where yang brightness arrives, it is the chamber that governs killing, causing the hardening and graying [of autumn]. Where greater yang arrives, it is the chamber of cold, causing return and storage. This is the constant pattern of governing transformation.

Where reverting yin arrives, there is generation, and wind causes swaying. Where lesser yin arrives, there is flourishing, and forms become visible. Where greater yin arrives, there is transformation, and clouds bring rain. Where lesser yang arrives, there is growth, and things become lush and vivid. Where yang brightness arrives, there is gathering-in, and fog and dew appear. Where greater yang arrives, there is storage, and all things become tightly sealed. This is the constant pattern of qi transformation.

Where reverting yin arrives, wind is generated, ending in sternness. Where lesser yin arrives, heat is generated, with cold appearing within. Where greater yin arrives, dampness is generated, ending in pouring rain. Where lesser yang arrives, fire is generated, ending in steaming sultriness. Where yang brightness arrives, dryness is generated, ending in coolness. Where greater yang arrives, cold is generated, with warmth appearing within. This is the constant pattern of virtuous transformation. Where reverting yin arrives, the furred creatures transform [flourish]; where lesser yin arrives, the feathered creatures transform; where greater yin arrives, the bare-skinned creatures transform; where lesser yang arrives, the feathered creatures transform; where yang brightness arrives, the shelled creatures transform; where greater yang arrives, the scaled creatures transform. This is the constant pattern of virtuous transformation.

Where reverting yin arrives, there is the transformation of generation; where lesser yin arrives, there is the transformation of flourishing; where greater yin arrives, there is the transformation of moistening; where lesser yang arrives, there is the transformation of luxuriance; where yang brightness arrives, there is the transformation of hardening; where greater yang arrives, there is the transformation of storage. This is the constant pattern of the spreading of governance.

Where reverting yin arrives, there is whirling, furious [wind], and severe coolness; where lesser yin arrives, there is great warmth alternating with cold; where greater yin arrives, there is thunder, sudden downpour, and fierce wind; where lesser yang arrives, there is whirling wind, scorching blaze, and congealing frost; where yang brightness arrives, there is scattering, falling, and warmth; where greater yang arrives, there is cold snow, ice, hail, and white dust. This is the constant pattern of qi change.

Where reverting yin arrives, there is stirring movement, advancing and following. Where lesser yin arrives, there is lofty brilliance and blazing, and dusky glow. Where greater yin arrives, there is deep overcast, white dust, and gloom. Where lesser yang arrives, there is radiant brightness, rosy clouds, and dusky glow. Where yang brightness arrives, there is smoke and dust, frost, cutting severity, and mournful sighing wind. Where greater yang arrives, there is hardness and firmness, sharp awns, and upright standing [of frost-stiffened plants]. This is the constant pattern of the circulation of ordinances.

Where reverting yin arrives, there is urgency within the abdomen; where lesser yin arrives, there are sores, rashes, and fever; where greater yin arrives, there is accumulated fluid with focal obstruction; where lesser yang arrives, there is sneezing, vomiting, and sores; where yang brightness arrives, there is floating vacuity; where greater yang arrives, there is difficulty in bending and stretching. This is the constant pattern of disease. Where reverting yin arrives, there is pain in the limbs; where lesser yin arrives, there is fright and confusion, aversion to cold, shivering, and delirious raving; where greater yin arrives, there is accumulation and fullness; where lesser yang arrives, there is fright and agitation, dizziness, and sudden illness; where yang brightness arrives, there is disease of the nose, sacrum, groin, thighs, knees, hips, calves, shins, and feet; where greater yang arrives, there is lower back pain. This is the constant pattern of disease. Where reverting yin arrives, there is contracture and twisting; where lesser yin arrives, there is sorrow, wild raving, and nosebleed; where greater yin arrives, there is fullness in the middle, sudden turmoil disorder with vomiting and diarrhea; where lesser yang arrives, there is throat obstruction, ringing in the ears, and retching; where yang brightness arrives, there is chapping and peeling of the skin; where greater yang arrives, there is night sweating and tetany. This is the constant pattern of disease. Where reverting yin arrives, there is rib-side pain with vomiting and diarrhea; where lesser yin arrives, there is [inappropriate] talking and laughing; where greater yin arrives, there is heaviness and puffy swelling; where lesser yang arrives, there is sudden pouring diarrhea, twitching, and sudden death; where yang brightness arrives, there is nasal obstruction and sneezing; where greater yang arrives, there is uncontrolled flux and diarrhea. This is the constant pattern of disease.

In all these twelve changes, virtue answers to virtue, transformation answers to transformation, governance answers to governance, and ordinance answers to ordinance; where the qi is high, [its effect] is high; where the qi is low, [its effect] is low; where the qi is behind, [its effect] is behind; where the qi is in front, [its effect] is in front; where the qi is central, [its effect] is central; where the qi is external, [its effect] is external — this is the constancy of position. Thus when wind prevails there is movement; when heat prevails there is swelling; when dryness prevails there is desiccation; when cold prevails there is floating [edema]; when dampness prevails there is sloppy diarrhea, and in severe cases urinary blockage with puffy swelling — one speaks of these changes according to wherever the qi resides.

The Emperor said: I wish to hear of their application. Qibo said: The application of the six qi each resorts to what it cannot overcome in order to bring about transformation. Thus the rain-transformation of greater yin is applied to greater yang; the cold-transformation of greater yang is applied to lesser yin; the heat-transformation of lesser yin is applied to yang brightness; the dryness-transformation of yang brightness is applied to reverting yin; and the wind-transformation of reverting yin is applied to greater yin — each is named according to where it resides, so that it may be verified. The Emperor asked: What of when [a qi] obtains its own position? Qibo said: When it obtains its own position, this is the constant transformation. The Emperor said: I wish to hear where these positions lie. Qibo said: Once its position is designated and its direction fixed, the month [of its governance] can be known. The Emperor asked: What of the fullness and emptiness of the qi in the six positions? Qibo said: The greater and the lesser differ: the greater arrives slowly and steadily, while the lesser arrives suddenly and passes away quickly. The Emperor asked: What of the fullness and emptiness of the qi of heaven and earth? Qibo said: When the qi of heaven is insufficient, the qi of earth follows it; when the qi of earth is insufficient, the qi of heaven follows it; the movement-qi resides between the two and habitually precedes them. It shuns what it cannot overcome and returns to what harmonizes with it, and disease arises as the movement-qi follows and returns accordingly. Thus when the upper [qi] prevails, the qi of heaven descends downward; when the lower [qi] prevails, the qi of earth shifts upward; the degree of deviation varies with the magnitude — a slight [imbalance] produces a small deviation, a severe one produces a great deviation; when severe, the position itself changes and the qi-intersection shifts, whereupon great changes arise and disease breaks out. The Essential Compendium says: severe [deviation] is reckoned at five parts, slight [deviation] at seven parts — its degree of deviation may thus be discerned. This is what is meant.

The Emperor said: Good. The discourse states that in [seasons of] heat one must not violate [the avoidance of] heat, and in [seasons of] cold one must not violate [the avoidance of] cold; yet I wish [at times] not to avoid cold, and not to avoid heat — how is this [reconciled]? Qibo said: How thorough is this question! In releasing the exterior, one need not avoid heat; in attacking the interior, one need not avoid cold. The Emperor asked: What of violating [these avoidances] when neither releasing nor attacking? Qibo said: Then cold and heat will damage the interior, and the disease will only worsen. The Emperor said: I wish to hear what happens in one who has no disease. Qibo said: In one without disease, [such violation] will generate it; in one who already has disease, it will aggravate it. The Emperor asked: What is meant by 'generate'? Qibo said: If one does not avoid heat, heat will arrive; if one does not avoid cold, cold will arrive. When cold arrives, hardness, obstruction, abdominal fullness, urgent pain, and diarrhea will result. When heat arrives, fever, vomiting and diarrhea, sudden turmoil disorder, welling-abscesses and sores, clouded oppression with pouring discharge, twitching and swelling, retching, nasal obstruction, nosebleed, headache, alteration of the bones and joints, muscle pain, overflow and discharge of blood, and painful urinary blockage will result. The Emperor asked: How is this treated? Qibo said: One must always comply with the season; where violation [of the avoidance] is necessary, treat by overcoming [the excess]. The Yellow Emperor asked: In a pregnant woman, how does one apply toxic medicine? Qibo said: If there is due cause, no harm will come — indeed, no harm will come [to the fetus]. The Emperor said: I wish to hear what is meant by 'due cause.' Qibo said: Where there is a great accumulation or great mass, one may indeed attack it with such medicine, stopping once it has been reduced by more than half; to exceed this brings death. The Emperor said: Good. How does one treat severe stagnation? Qibo said: Wood stagnation should be freely vented; fire stagnation should be released outward; earth stagnation should be purged away; metal stagnation should be drained; water stagnation should be broken down — yet in all cases one must regulate the qi, breaking down what is excessive by means of what it dreads; this is what is called 'draining.' The Emperor asked: What of when there is a false [semblance]? Qibo said: Where the qi presents a false [semblance], there is no prohibition [against contrary treatment] — this refers to cases where the host qi is insufficient and the guest qi has become dominant. The Emperor said: Perfect indeed is the Way of the sage! The regulation of the great transformation and circulation of heaven and earth, the chronicle of their presiding governance, the administration of yin and yang, and the ordinances of cold and heat — who but master could master these? I beg to store this away in the Chamber of the Numinous Orchid, entitling it 'The Correct Chronicle of the Six Origins.' Without fasting and purification I dare not display it — let it be transmitted with the utmost care.

素问.title_en: Treatise on Needling Methods
paragraphs_en: Huangdi asked: "When ascent and descent fail to advance, and the intersection of qi undergoes change, sudden stagnation results — this I already understand. But how can living beings be saved in advance, so that this may be averted?" Qibo bowed his head twice and replied: "How illuminating is this question! I have heard you speak of the celestial origin with such clarity that now we must fully investigate the methods of needling, so as to break up stagnation and support the movement, to tonify what is weak and preserve what is genuine, to drain what is in excess, and thereby remove this affliction." Huangdi said: "I wish to hear it in full." Qibo said: "When ascent fails to advance, great calamity follows. When wood wishes to ascend but Celestial Pillar obstructs and suppresses it, wood's stagnation, when it is about to break forth, must also await its proper time; one should needle the Well point of the Foot Jueyin channel. When fire wishes to ascend but Celestial Canopy obstructs and suppresses it, fire's stagnation, when about to break forth, must likewise await its time; for both sovereign fire and ministerial fire, needle the Spring point of the Pericardium channel. When earth wishes to ascend but Celestial Thoroughfare obstructs and suppresses it, earth's stagnation, when about to break forth, must likewise await its time; one should needle the Stream point of the Foot Taiyin channel. When metal wishes to ascend but Celestial Efflorescence obstructs and suppresses it, metal's stagnation, when about to break forth, must likewise await its time; one should needle the River point of the Hand Taiyin channel. When water wishes to ascend but Celestial Inner obstructs and suppresses it, water's stagnation, when about to break forth, must likewise await its time; one should needle the Sea point of the Foot Shaoyin channel."

Huangdi said: "Regarding ascent failing to advance, one can prepare in advance; I wish to hear about descent, so that it too may be guarded against beforehand." Qibo said: "Having understood ascent, one must also comprehend descent. The way of ascent and descent can both be treated in advance. When wood wishes to descend but Earthly Xiao obstructs and suppresses it, descent fails to enter; when suppression causes stagnation to break forth, dispersal allows it to regain its position. When descent causes stagnation to break forth suddenly, it is like the pattern of Celestial Idleness awaiting its time. When descent fails to go down, the stagnation may become acute; in descending, one may break what it overcomes. One should needle the Well point of the Hand Taiyin channel, and the Sea point of the Hand Yangming channel. When fire wishes to descend but Earthly Mystery obstructs and suppresses it, descent fails to enter; when suppression causes stagnation to break forth, dispersal will resolve it. One should break what it overcomes, so as to disperse its stagnation; needle the Well point of the Foot Shaoyin channel, and the Sea point of the Foot Taiyang channel. When earth wishes to descend but Earthly Verdant obstructs and suppresses it, descent fails to go down; when suppression causes stagnation to break forth, dispersal allows it to enter. One should break what overcomes it, so as to disperse its stagnation; needle the Well point of the Foot Jueyin channel, and the Sea point of the Foot Shaoyang channel. When metal wishes to descend but Earthly Vermilion obstructs and suppresses it, descent fails to go down; when the dispersal of suppressed stagnation breaks forth, dispersal allows it to enter. One should break what overcomes it, so as to disperse its stagnation; needle the Well point of the Pericardium channel, and the Sea point of the Hand Shaoyang channel. When water wishes to descend but Earthly Mound obstructs and suppresses it, descent fails to go down; when suppression causes stagnation to break forth, dispersal allows it to enter. One should break its earth phase, so as to disperse its stagnation; needle the Well point of the Foot Taiyin channel, and the Sea point of the Foot Yangming channel."

Huangdi said: "In the arrival of the five movements, there is precedence and sequence, ascent and descent, coming and going, and there is a phase that receives and suppresses another — may I hear of this?" Regarding the needling method, Qibo said: "One should select the source of transformation. Therefore, in cases of excess, select it to drain; in cases of insufficiency, supplement it. In excess, select it to drain, and next suppress its stagnation, selecting the source of transformation of that movement, so as to break the stagnant qi. In insufficiency, support and supplement it, so as to bolster the movement's qi and avoid vacuity evil. The methods of supplementing and selecting are set forth in the Secret Sayings."

Huangdi asked: "Regarding the needling of ascent and descent, I now know the essentials. I wish to hear about when the governor of heaven fails to shift into its proper position, causing the governance of transformation to lose its normal rule, so that the transformations of the myriad things may all go awry. Since this becomes disease among the people, may it be prevented in advance? Wishing to aid all living beings, I wish to hear your explanation." Qibo bowed his head twice and said: "How thorough is this question! You speak of the utmost principle; your sagely thoughts are compassionate, wishing to aid all living beings. I shall therefore set forth this Way in full, so as to expound its subtleties. When Taiyang reasserts its spread, then Jueyin fails to shift into position; when it fails to shift into position, qi becomes blocked above — one should drain the Spring point of the Foot Jueyin channel. When Jueyin reasserts its spread, then Shaoyin fails to shift into position; when it fails to shift into position, qi becomes blocked above — one should needle the Spring point of the Pericardium channel. When Shaoyin reasserts its spread, then Taiyin fails to shift into position; when it fails to shift into position, qi lingers above — one should needle the Spring point of the Foot Taiyin channel. When Taiyin reasserts its spread, then Shaoyang fails to shift into position; when it fails to shift into position, qi is blocked and does not flow through — one should needle the Spring point of the Hand Shaoyang channel. When Shaoyang reasserts its spread, then Yangming fails to shift into position; when it fails to shift into position, qi does not flow through above — one should needle the Spring point of the Hand Taiyin channel. When Yangming reasserts its spread, then Taiyang fails to shift into position; when it fails to shift into position, its qi becomes blocked once more — one should needle the Spring point of the Foot Shaoyin channel."

Huangdi said: "Regarding the qi failing to shift into position and advance, I now understand the essentials for freeing it. I wish to hear about the qi failing to retreat — wishing to break its excess and prevent transgression, can this be made clear?" Qibo said: "When qi passes its due measure and has surplus, it acts again to spread its governance — this is called 'not passing beyond its position.' It causes the earthly qi to be unable to transform in succession, and the new governor of heaven cannot yet shift into position; therefore the old qi again spreads its transforming mandate as before. In years of Si and Hai, the celestial number has a surplus, so Jueyin does not retreat from its position; wind moves above, and wood's transformation spreads over heaven — one should needle the Sea point of the Foot Jueyin channel. In years of Zi and Wu, the celestial number has a surplus, so Shaoyin does not retreat from its position; heat moves above, and fire's surplus transformation spreads over heaven — one should needle the Sea point of the Hand Jueyin channel. In years of Chou and Wei, the celestial number has a surplus, so Taiyin does not retreat from its position; dampness moves above, and rain's transformation spreads over heaven — one should needle the Sea point of the Foot Taiyin channel. In years of Yin and Shen, the celestial number has a surplus, so Shaoyang does not retreat from its position; heat moves above, and fire's transformation spreads over heaven — one should needle the Sea point of the Hand Shaoyang channel. In years of Mao and You, the celestial number has a surplus, so Yangming does not retreat from its position; metal moves above, and dryness's transformation spreads over heaven — one should needle the Sea point of the Hand Taiyin channel. In years of Chen and Xu, the celestial number has a surplus, so Taiyang does not retreat from its position; cold moves above, and the frigid transformation of water spreads over heaven — one should needle the Sea point of the Foot Shaoyin channel. Thus when the qi of heaven and earth is contrary, it transforms into disease among the people; by needling according to this method, the affliction may be settled in advance."

Huangdi asked: "When the hard and soft stems lose their hold on their positions, does this make the qi of the celestial movement entirely vacuous? And when this becomes disease among the people, can it be settled?" Qibo said: "How profound is this question! To clarify its deep meaning: when heaven and earth shift out of turn, in three years this transforms into pestilence. This is what is meant by 'the root can be seen, and there must be a door of escape.'"

Suppose in a Jiazi year the hard and soft stems lose their hold: the hard has not attained correctness, the soft stands alone and is deficient, the sequence of the seasons fails to give its mandate, and the pitch-pipes and tones fail to correspond. If this continues for three years, it transforms into a great pestilence. Examine carefully whether it is slight or severe, observe whether it is shallow or deep; when it is about to arrive, one may needle. In needling, first tonify the Kidney Shu point; then after three days, one may needle the Stream point of the Foot Taiyin channel. There is also the case where the lower position of Jimao fails to arrive, and Jiazi stands alone; after three years this becomes earth pestilence. The method of tonifying and draining is exactly the same as for Jiazi. After the needling is finished, one must not travel at night or go on long journeys; keep clean for seven days, in purity and abstinence. For anyone who has long-standing kidney disease, at the hour of Yin, facing south, purify the spirit without letting it be disturbed, hold the breath without breathing seven times, stretching the neck to swallow the qi and guide it downward as though swallowing something very hard; after doing this seven times, take in the saliva beneath the tongue repeatedly, without limit.

Suppose in a Bingyin year the hard and soft lose their hold: above, the hard stem loses its hold; below, the soft cannot govern alone. The water movement in the middle is not in excess, so one cannot hold to the fixed rule to determine it. Its spread over heaven has a surplus, yet it loses the correct hold above; heaven and earth fail to unite, and the pitch-pipes and tones differ. Thus the celestial movement loses its sequence, and after three years this transforms into pestilence. Examine carefully whether it is slight or severe; the discrepancy has degrees, great or small. If it comes slowly, it arrives after three years; if it comes severely, it arrives within the first three years. One should first tonify the Heart Shu point; then after five days, one may needle the Sea point of the kidney. There is also the case of the lower earthly Jiazi position, where Xinsi, the soft, fails to attach to the hard; this is likewise called 'losing the hold.' Then the earthly movement is entirely vacuous, and after three years this transforms into water pestilence. The needling method is entirely the same as above. After the needling is finished, take care against great joy or excessive desire and emotion within; if this is not guarded against, the qi will disperse again. Keep quiet for seven days; when the heart is to be made replete, let there be little contemplation.

Suppose in a Gengchen year the hard and soft lose their hold: the upper position loses its hold, and the lower position has no union. Since Yi and Geng govern the metal movement, they do not summon each other; its spread over heaven has not yet retreated, while the central movement's dominance arrives — upper and lower cross one another in disorder. This is called 'losing the hold.' The pitch-pipes Guxian and Linzhong, and the note Shang, fail to correspond. Thus the celestial movement's transformation changes, and in three years this transforms into a great pestilence. Examine carefully the celestial number; the discrepancy is slight or severe — if slight, it is slight and arrives in three years; if severe, it is severe and arrives in three years. One should first tonify the Liver Shu point; then after three days, one may needle the River point of the lung. After needling, keep the spirit quiet for seven days; take care not to become greatly angry, for anger will surely cause the genuine qi to retreat and disperse. There is also the case of the lower earthly Jiazi position, where Yiwei loses its hold: then Yi, the soft stem, fails, and Geng above governs alone. This too is called 'losing the hold'; then it stands alone in governance. In three years this transforms into pestilence, called metal pestilence, and its arrival awaits its proper time. By examining the gradations of the earthly numbers, and likewise inferring whether it is slight or severe, one may know whether it will be slow or swift. In all positions where Yi and Geng lose their hold, the needling method is the same; when the liver is to be calmed, one must avoid anger.

Suppose in a Renwu year the hard and soft lose their hold: above, Ren has not shifted into position; below, Ding stands alone. Although this is a yang year, the deficiency is not uniform. Upper and lower lose their hold, and their mutual summoning has its season; the discrepancy, slight or severe, each has its own number. The two Jue pitches among the pitch-pipes lose harmony; there are days when the notes coincide, and whether slight or severe becomes apparent — in three years, a great pestilence. One should needle the Spleen Shu point; then after three days, one may needle the Well point of the liver. After needling, keep the spirit quiet for seven days; do not become greatly drunk or indulge in song and pleasure, for this will cause the qi to disperse again. Also do not eat to excess, and do not eat raw foods. When one wishes the spleen to become replete, let the qi not stagnate from overeating; do not sit for long periods; do not eat overly sour foods, nor any raw foods — sweet and bland foods are suitable. There is also the case of the earthly Jiazi position, where Dingyou loses its hold on its position and has not obtained central governance; then the qi does not occupy its proper position, and below it does not unite with Ren in reverent accord. This too is called 'losing the hold,' not 'united in virtue.' Thus the soft fails to attach to the hard, and the earthly movement fails to unite; in three years this transforms into pestilence. Its needling method is entirely the same as that for wood pestilence.

Suppose in a Wushen year the hard and soft lose their hold: although Wu and Gui govern the fire movement, in a yang year it is not in excess. Above, it loses its hardness; the soft earth governs alone, and its qi is not correct — hence evil comes to intrude. Their positions shift out of turn, with discrepancies shallow or deep. When the pestilence is about to arrive and the union is about to occur, the pitch-pipes first coincide. Thus the celestial movement loses its timing, and within three years, fire pestilence arrives. One should needle the Lung Shu point. After needling, keep the spirit quiet for seven days; do not indulge in great grief or sorrow, for grief and sorrow will stir the lung, causing the genuine qi to disperse again. For one who wishes to make the lung replete, the essential point lies in resting the breath. There is also the case of the earthly Jiazi position, where Guihai loses its hold: then the soft loses its hold on position, and above loses its hardness; this too is called Wu and Gui failing to unite in virtue. Then the movement and the earth are both vacuous, and after three years this transforms into pestilence, called fire pestilence.

Therefore, in establishing the five earthly years to clarify the loss of hold, and in needling according to this method — thus 'epidemic' and 'pestilence' are but names for the loss of hold of the hard and soft above and below, and in the end they belong to a single body. Thus the methods of needling epidemics number only five; they gather together the losses of hold in all the positions, and so are governed and unified under the five phases alone.

Huangdi said: "I have heard that when the five epidemics arrive, they all spread by contagion, without regard to old or young, the symptoms of the disease being similar. If treatment is not given, how can one avoid being infected by transmission?" Qibo said: "One who is not infected is one in whom the correct qi is preserved within, so that evil cannot intrude. To avoid the toxic qi: it comes from the Celestial Female portal, and may likewise be sent back out through that same path. When qi issues forth from the brain, evil cannot intrude. When qi issues forth from the brain — before entering the room, first visualize the heart as bright as the sun. When about to enter a room where pestilence is present, first visualize green qi issuing from the liver, moving leftward to the east, transforming into forest and trees. Next visualize white qi issuing from the lung, moving rightward to the west, transforming into halberds and armor. Next visualize red qi issuing from the heart, moving south and upward, transforming into blazing flame. Next visualize black qi issuing from the kidney, moving north and downward, transforming into water. Next visualize yellow qi issuing from the spleen, abiding in the center, transforming into earth. When this protection of the body by the five qi is complete, visualize above the head a radiance like the resplendent brilliance of the Northern Dipper; only then may one enter the room of pestilence."

There is another method: on the day of the spring equinox, before the sun rises, induce vomiting. There is yet another method: after the day of Rain Water, bathe three times and use medicine to induce sweating. There is yet another method, the recipe for the Small Golden Elixir: cinnabar, two liang; realgar ground in water, one liang; orpiment in leaf form, one liang; purple gold, half a liang. Combine all together in a sealed vessel; seal it well on the outside, dig one foot into the earth and tamp the ground firm to set it in. Do not use a furnace, nor need any preparation of medicine beforehand — use twenty catties of fire to calcine it for seven full days; then wait seven days for it to cool before retrieving it. The next day, take the vessel out, bury the medicine in the earth for seven days, then take it out and grind it, following the sun's course, for three days. Refine it with white sand honey into pills the size of firmiana seeds. Each day, facing east, inhale one mouthful of the sun's radiant qi, then swallow one pill with ice water, taking it down together with the qi. Taking ten pills, one will not be affected by pestilence.

Huangdi asked: "When a person is vacuous, the spirit wanders and loses its hold on its position, allowing ghosts and spirits to intrude from outside, which leads to premature death. How may one preserve the genuine qi? I wish to hear the needling method." Qibo bowed his head twice and said: "How illuminating is this question! When it is said that the spirit shifts and loses its hold, although the person remains in his body, this alone does not cause death; but if evil then intrudes, it causes life to be cut short. For instance, when Jueyin loses its hold, heaven becomes vacuous; if a person's liver qi is also vacuous, and he is affected by heaven's doubled vacuity, then the ethereal soul wanders above, and evil intrudes upon the great qi of Jueyin. If the body is still warm, one may still needle him: needle the Source point of the Foot Shaoyang channel, then needle the Liver Shu point. If a person's heart is vacuous with disease, and he further encounters the loss of hold of the sovereign and ministerial fires governing heaven, so that he is affected by a triple vacuity, and encounters an insufficiency of fire, then the Black Corpse Ghost assails him, causing sudden death. One may needle the Source point of the Hand Shaoyang channel, then needle the Heart Shu point. If a person has spleen disease, and further encounters the loss of hold of Taiyin governing heaven, so that he is affected by a triple vacuity, and encounters an insufficiency of earth, then the Green Corpse Ghost assails the person, causing sudden death. One may needle the Source point of the Foot Yangming channel, then needle the Spleen Shu point. If a person has lung disease, and encounters the loss of hold of Yangming governing heaven, so that he is affected by a triple vacuity, and encounters an insufficiency of metal, then the Red Corpse Ghost intrudes upon the person, causing sudden death. One may needle the Source point of the Hand Yangming channel, then needle the Lung Shu point. If a person has kidney disease, and further encounters the loss of hold of Taiyang governing heaven, so that he is affected by a triple vacuity, and further encounters a year of insufficient water movement, then the Yellow Corpse Ghost intrudes upon and assails the person's correct qi, drawing out his spirit and souls, resulting in sudden death. One may needle the Source point of the Foot Taiyang channel, and needle the Shu point of the Foot Shaoyang channel."

Huangdi asked: "Regarding the mutual employment of the twelve viscera, when the spirit loses its position, causing the brilliance of the spirit to be incomplete, and there is fear that evil will intrude and offend — this may be treated by needling. I wish to hear its essentials." Qibo bowed his head twice and said: "How thorough is this — you ask of the ultimate principle, the true ancestral Way. Were it not for a sagely sovereign, who could fathom this source? This is what is called 'qi and spirit uniting with the Way, in accord and correspondence with high heaven.' The heart is the official of the sovereign and ruler; spirit and brightness issue from it. One may needle the Source point of the Hand Shaoyin channel. The lung is the official of the chancellor and mentor; regulation and moderation issue from it. One may needle the Source point of the Hand Taiyin channel. The liver is the official of the general; planning and deliberation issue from it. One may needle the Source point of the Foot Jueyin channel. The gallbladder is the official of impartial justice; decision and judgment issue from it. One may needle the Source point of the Foot Shaoyang channel. The chest center is the official of minister and envoy; joy and pleasure issue from it. One may needle the Spring point of the Pericardium channel. The spleen is the official of remonstrance and counsel; comprehensive knowledge issues from it. One may needle the Source point of the spleen. The stomach is the official of the granaries and storehouses; the five flavors issue from it. One may needle the Source point of the stomach. The large intestine is the official of transmission and conduction; transformation and change issue from it. One may needle the Source point of the large intestine. The small intestine is the official of reception and containment; the transformation of substances issues from it. One may needle the Source point of the small intestine. The kidney is the official of vigor and strength; skill and ability issue from it. Needle the Source point of the kidney. The triple burner is the official of waterways and channels; the passage of water issues from it. Needle the Source point of the triple burner. The bladder is the official of the regional capital; fluids and humors are stored in it, and through the transformation of qi they can be discharged. Needle the Source point of the bladder. In all, these twelve officials must not lose their mutual accord. Therefore the needling method carries the intent of preserving the spirit whole and nurturing what is genuine; there is also, within the method, the Way of cultivating what is genuine — this is not merely the treatment of disease, but essentially the cultivation and harmonizing of the spirit. The Way values constant preservation: to tonify the spirit and secure the root, so that essence and qi do not disperse, and the spirit's guard is not divided. Thus, when the spirit maintains its guard and does not depart, one likewise preserves what is genuine in its entirety. If a person's spirit fails to maintain its guard, he cannot attain the utmost genuineness. The essential point of the utmost genuineness lies in the celestial mystery: when the spirit guards the celestial breath, it returns once more into the original source — this is called 'returning to the ancestral root.'"

素问.title_en: Discourse on the Root of Disease
paragraphs_en: Huangdi asked: I already understand the nine obstructions of the celestial origin; I wish now to hear about the interchange of qi — what is meant by "losing its hold"? Qi Bo replied: It refers to the ascending and descending of qi above and below, its shifting into correct position and its retreating from position, each governed by its own canonical principles; when above and below each fails to advance, this is called "losing its hold." Thus when the interchange of qi loses its proper place, the interchange itself is altered, and this alteration is not ordinary — the four seasons lose their sequence, the transformations of the myriad things become unsettled, and this is transformed into disease among the people.
The Emperor said: I wish to hear the reason why ascent and descent fail to advance. When the interchange of qi undergoes alteration, how can this be clearly known?
